original: US launches Solar Explorer 2 to study the Sun
Nature documentary "Planet Earth" narrated byDavid Attenboroughpremieres on the BBC
Beatrice Chebet, Kenyan athlete (Olympic gold 5,000/10,000m 2024; 10,000m WR 28:54.14 2024), born in Kericho, Kenya
Frank Carauna, becomes 1st to bowl 2 successive perfect 300 games
Bob Seagren pole vaults 5.19m indoor world record

First performance in 3,000 years of world's oldest known song "Hymn to Nikkal" a 3,400 year old Hurrian hymn to moon god Nikkal from Ugarit in Syria, played at Berkeley University by Anne Kilmer and Richard Crocker
Russian spacecraft Venera 14 lands on Venus sends back data
ExplorerErnest Shackleton's ship Endurance, which sank in 1915, is rediscovered after 107 years in excellent condition in the Weddell Sea, Antarctica
US rocket flies record 4800 kph to 126km height
Voyager I's closest approach to Jupiter (172,000 miles)
Earth satellites record gamma rays from remnants of supernova N-49
Major study into the MMR vaccine involving over 650,000 children in Denmark finds it does not increase the risk of autism
Second-ever person "cured" of HIV after stem cell transplant treatment in London, England results published in "Nature"
Robert L Curbeam Jr, astronaut (STS 85, sk: 99), born in Baltimore, Maryland
Spitfire makes its 1st flight (Eastleigh Aerodrome in Southampton)
Dubnium atoms are first detected conclusively.
1st performance ofWalter Piston's 8th Symphony (his last), by the Boston Symphony, conducted by Erich Leinsdorf
Atlanta Mayor Ivan Allen Jr, announces a baseball team is moving there
Manufacture of bone tools by ancient humans is pushed back a million years by the discovery of a 1.5 million year old cache of bone tools in Olduvai Gorge, Tanzania
Elvis Presleymakes his 1st TV appearance on a local video broadcast of the popular radio program "Louisiana Hayride" from the Shreveport Municipal Auditorium in Shreveport, Louisiana
Maria do Carmo Geronimo, Brazilian maid, former slave and oldest Brazilian (lived to be at about 129), born in Carmo de Minas, Brazil (d. 2000)
Lynn Margulis, American biologist (serial endosymbiotic theory), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 2011)
Mikayla Raines, American wildlife rehabilitator and founder of Save a Fox Rescue, born in Burnsville, Minnesota (d. 2025)
The Beatles record "From Me to You" and "Thank You Girl"
1st ascent of Mt Erebus, Antarctica
Computing's top prize, the Turing Award is given to American researchers Andrew Barto and Richard Sutton for their work on reinforcement learning, a cornerstone of AI technology
American engineerGeorge Westinghousepatents triple air brake for trains
Nikola Tesladescribes the process of the ball lightning formation in Electrical World and Engineer

Gerardus Mercator, Flemish geographer, globe maker, and cartographer (introduced the term atlas, added rhumb lines to aid navigation by sea, Mercator 1569 world map), born in Rupelmonde, Flanders (d. 1594)
Reggie Miller (Indiana Pacers) begins NBA free throw streak of 52 games
Jan van der Heyden, Dutch painter and inventor (fire hose; street lighting), born in Gorinchem, South Holland (d. 1712)
South Sea dike in Amsterdam breaks after storm
1st premature baby health law in US (Chicago)
Sgt Bilko satirizes Elvis Presley (Elvis Pelvin)
The Aquatic Ape Hypothesis originates when Alister Hardy publicly announces his idea that ape-human divergence may have been due to a coastal phase.
Willcom announces the world’s smallest mobile phone, weighing 32 grams
1st performance ofLudwig van Beethoven's 4th Symphony in B
World première ofDmitri Shostakovich' 7th Symphony in Kuybyshev, Russia
"Stairway to Heaven" by Led Zeppelin is first performed live at Ulster Hall, Belfast, byRobert Plant,Jimmy Page,John Bonham, andJohn Paul Jones
NHL New York IslandersMike Bossyis 1st to score 50 goals in 8 straight seasons

British soldiers kill five men in a crowd throwing snowballs, stones, and sticks at them. African AmericanCrispus Attucksis the first to die and is later held up as an early Black martyr. The Boston Massacre or Incident on King Street galvanizes anti-British feelings.
Frits Slomp, Dutch pastor and resistance fighter (aided people in hiding during WWII), born in Ruinerwold, Netherlands (d. 1978)
Leontine T. Kelly, 1st African American female bishop (United Methodist Church), born in Georgetown, Washington D.C. (d. 2012)
Charlie Reid, Scottish rock guitarist, songwriter, and vocalist (The Proclaimers - "I'm Gonna Be (500 Miles)"), born in Leith, Scotland
Jake Lloyd, American actor (The Phantom Menace), born in Fort Collins, Colorado
Justin Fields, American NFL football quarterback (Chicago Bears), born in Kennesaw, Georgia
English KingHenry VIIgrantsJohn Cabot(Giovanni Caboto) a commission to explore for new lands
Bugatti announces the most expensive new car ever made - the La Voiture Noire costing €16.7 million (almost $19 million), only one will be made
Gandhi& British viceroy Lord Irwin sign pact in London - marks end of the Civil Disobedience Movement in India
Paul Radmilovic, Welsh swimmer (Olympic gold GB 4×200m freestyle 1908; gold GB water polo 1908, 12, 20), born in Cardiff, Wales (d. 1968)
Heitor Villa-Lobos, Brazilian cellist, guitarist, and conductor, and composer (Bachianas Brasileiras), born in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil (d. 1959)
Konstantin Sergeyev, Russian ballet dancer (Romeo and Juliet) and Director of the Kirov Ballet, born in St. Petersburg, Russia (d. 1992)[1]
Laurent Schwartz, French mathematician, 1950 Fields Medal for his theory of distributions, born in Paris (d. 2002)
İsmail Ogan, Turkish freestyle wrestler (Olympic gold 78kg 1964, silver 1960), born in Macun, Turkey (d. 2022)
Philip K Chapman, Australian astronaut (Apollo 14 support), born in Melbourne, Victoria (d. 2021)
Alan Clark, British keyboardist (Dire Straits, 1980-95 - "Money for Nothing"; Tina Turner; Eric Clapton), producer, and composer, born in County Durham, England
Craig Reid, Scottish rock singer-songwriter (The Proclaimers - "I'm Gonna Be (500 Miles)"), born in Leith, Scotland
Michael Irvin, American Pro Football HOF wide receiver (Super Bowl 1992, 93, 95; Dallas Cowboys; 5 × Pro Bowl) and broadcaster (ESPN, NFL Network), born in Fort Lauderdale, Florida
Zach Williams, American Christian rock musician ("There was Jesus"), born in Pensacola, Florida
Daniil Trifonov, Russian Grammy Award-winning classical pianist, born in Nizhny Novgorod, Soviet Union
